subsection.title: Vector field in the plane
subsection.p: A vector field in the plane has the form \mathbf F(x,y)=(P(x,y),Q(x,y)).
On a grid:

program.input:
x = np.linspace(a, b, 25) y = np.linspace(c, d, 25) X, Y = np.meshgrid(x, y)
P = Pfun(X, Y) Q = Qfun(X, Y)
plt.quiver(X, Y, P, Q) plt.axis("equal") plt.xlabel("x") plt.ylabel("y") plt.show()

subsection.title: Normalized arrows
subsection.p: Sometimes large arrows hide the pattern. Normalize arrows:

program.input:
speed = np.sqrt(P**2 + Q**2) P0 = P / (speed + 1e-12) Q0 = Q / (speed + 1e-12)
plt.quiver(X, Y, P0, Q0) plt.axis("equal") plt.show()

subsection.p: This shows direction but not magnitude.

subsection.title: Stream plot
subsection.p: Streamlines solve \mathbf r'(t)=\mathbf F(\mathbf r(t)).

program.input: plt.streamplot(X, Y, P, Q) plt.axis("equal") plt.show()

subsection.title: Common vector fields
subsection.p: Constant field: \mathbf F(x,y)=(1,0).
Radial source: \mathbf F(x,y)=(x,y).
Radial sink: \mathbf F(x,y)=(-x,-y).
Rotation: \mathbf F(x,y)=(-y,x).
Saddle field: \mathbf F(x,y)=(x,-y).
Inverse radial field: \mathbf F(x,y)=\frac{(x,y)}{x^2+y^2}.

subsection.title: Gradient field
subsection.p: For f=f(x,y), \nabla f=(f_x,f_y).
Numerically, use np.gradient if f is sampled on a grid:

program.input:
Z = f(X, Y)
dy = y[1] - y[0] dx = x[1] - x[0]
Z_y, Z_x = np.gradient(Z, dy, dx)
plt.quiver(X, Y, Z_x, Z_y) plt.contour(X, Y, Z, levels=20) plt.axis("equal") plt.show()

subsection.title: Vector field in space
subsection.p: A vector field in space has the form \mathbf F(x,y,z)=(P,Q,R).
A 3-D quiver plot:

program.input:
fig = plt.figure() ax = fig.add_subplot(projection="3d")
ax.quiver(X, Y, Z, P, Q, R)
plt.show()

subsection.p: Use fewer grid points in 3-D. Too many arrows quickly become unreadable.

subsection.title: Field line through one point
subsection.p: For \mathbf r'(t)=\mathbf F(\mathbf r(t)), use an ODE solver:

program.input:
from scipy.integrate import solve_ivp
def ode(t, r): x, y = r return [Pfun(x, y), Qfun(x, y)]
sol = solve_ivp(ode, [t0, t1], [x0, y0], max_step=0.02)
plt.plot(sol.y[0], sol.y[1]) plt.axis("equal") plt.show()

subsection.title: Common warnings
subsection.p: Arrow length in a plot may be automatically rescaled.
A normalized quiver plot hides speed.
A stream plot may hide sources, sinks, or singularities.
A vector field with a hole in its domain should not be plotted as if the hole were ordinary.
